contract_conformance.notes: W6 backend conforms to api.openapi.yaml (v1.1.0), providers.md (Usage capture / EM-067), event-log.md §7, and action-protocol.schema.json (v1.1.0). Verified by behavior (MockProvider + FastAPI TestClient + httpx-layer stub). READ ENDPOINTS: GET /api/events returns 200 EventRows carrying the exact §7 key set {seq,run_id,tick,sim_time,kind,actor_id,actor_type,target_id,profile,turn_id,text,payload,ts} and honors EVERY documented query param (from_tick,to_tick,kinds comma-list,actor_id,turn_id,after_seq,limit,order asc|desc default asc); /api/turns/{turn_id} returns the ordered turn chain (6 trace spans in order, one turn_id, seq-asc); /api/rules/history returns lifecycle entries with all documented keys; /api/relationships returns EventRows + honors agent_id/from/to_tick; /api/snapshots returns ascending [{tick}] incl. tick 0; /api/replay?tick= returns {base(nearest prior snapshot, tick<=T), events(folded forward, all tick<=T)}; /api/analytics returns all 11 §7 top-level keys incl. by_model + usage.by_profile (populated from llm_call rows). PER-ATTEMPT llm_call (EM-067): a forced schema-invalid action emits TWO llm_call rows (attempt 1+2) sharing one turn_id and resolves idle/failed; a success emits exactly one; OTel GenAI key set is exact on every row. USAGE: an openai-style usage block flows to adapter.last_usage (input/output tokens, finish_reason, latency) AND into the llm_call payload (gen_ai.usage.input_tokens=200/output_tokens=50, finish_reasons=['stop'], response.model passthrough), replacing the W5 nulls; Mock stays null. TRUNCATION (live-run fix): perceived_summary>600 / reasoning>1200 / memories_used item>160 are truncated in place to their caps and the turn still resolves to the chosen action (forage, outcome ok) NOT idle; a wrong-type memories_used (string) is dropped to null (turn still ok); structural args (bad move_to place, give to ghost target) still fail strictly to idle/outcome failed.

invariants.notes: GRACEFUL-DEGRADE invariant (the headline W6 read-API guarantee): with NO active run/repo, every read endpoint returns an empty 200 — /api/events,/api/turns/*,/api/rules/history,/api/relationships,/api/snapshots => [] ; /api/replay => {base:None,events:[]} ; /api/analytics => {} — NEVER a 500 (asserted with raise_server_exceptions=True so any 500 fails the test). PER-ATTEMPT CORRELATION invariant: parse-failure retry emits one llm_call per attempt, both stamped with the single mint-once turn_id; turn_start shares it; success path never spawns a phantom attempt-2 span. USAGE NULL-TOLERANCE invariant: real provider tokens are captured and surfaced while Mock's last_usage stays None and its llm_call tokens stay null, with the full OTel key set present in BOTH cases. TRUNCATION invariant: optional cosmetic/trace fields can never lose a whole turn (truncate-or-drop), while structural args (action/place/target/amount) remain strictly validated — the exact behavioral split the live-run fix guarantees. All assertions are real equalities on lengths/outcomes/payloads over the same TickLoop._execute_turn path the asyncio loop uses; deterministic across repeated runs (97 passed on two consecutive full-suite runs).

test_coverage.notes: Added backend/tests/test_w6.py: 18 new pytest cases, deterministic + offline, mirroring existing tests/*.py harness patterns (World/Router/Runtime/loop wiring; httpx threaded stub from test_openai_adapter.py; FastAPI TestClient). Coverage by group: (1) READ ENDPOINTS — 9 cases: events shape, all-filters, turn trace, rules history, relationships, snapshots, replay, analytics top-level keys, and no-active-run empty-200-never-500. The read endpoints are exercised over the real HTTP surface by binding the app's module-level singletons (reached via sys.modules['petridish.api.app'] because the package __init__ shadows the submodule with the FastAPI instance) to a pre-driven 30-turn in-memory mock run; originals are restored on teardown. (2) PER-ATTEMPT — 2 cases: forced-failure two rows / success one row. (3) USAGE — 3 cases: adapter.last_usage capture, end-to-end llm_call payload tokens through the loop, mock-null alongside real capture. (4) TRUNCATION — 4 cases: over-long truncation, wrong-type drop, bad place, bad target. Full suite: 97 passed (79 prior + 18 new), 0 failed.

security.notes: Low surface, local-first, single-user, no auth/PII (documented). W6-relevant strengths: every read endpoint uses parameterized SQL — the kinds IN-clause and all filters bind '?' params (no string-built SQL from query input); the comma-split kinds list is bound element-wise. Append-only source-of-truth is unchanged: reads cannot mutate the log. Token-usage capture is null-tolerant via _coerce_int (rejects bool, coerces str/float, None on garbage) so a malformed provider usage block cannot inject a non-int into analytics math. The no-active-run path fails closed to empty 200s rather than leaking a stack trace via 500. Not hardened for public-internet exposure; no new attack surface added by the tests. Held at 4 (not 5) only because the API remains unauthenticated by design — acceptable for the local-first scope but the reason it is not a 5.

issues: petridish/api/__init__.py does `from .app import app`, so `petridish.api.app` resolves to the FastAPI INSTANCE, not the submodule — the read-endpoint fixture must reach the module via sys.modules['petridish.api.app'] to rebind/restore the _world/_loop/_repo singletons. Documented in-test; not a defect, but a footgun for future test authors.

The read-endpoint tests bind the app's module-level singletons to a pre-driven run WITHOUT entering the FastAPI lifespan (no background asyncio task). This is intentional — the W6 read endpoints are pure reads over _repo/_loop._run_id — and keeps the HTTP-surface tests deterministic, but it means these tests do not cover lifespan startup wiring (already covered by test_api_routes.py).

USAGE capture is asserted for the openai adapter path (the live-run path, EM-067). Anthropic/Gemini token capture is implemented identically in adapters.py and unit-shaped the same way, but is not separately stubbed in this file; the openai stub is the representative live path per the brief.

gate_decision.reason: All 97 tests pass (18 new W6 + 79 prior regressions), zero failures, zero blockers, deterministic across two consecutive full-suite runs. No CRITICAL blocker. contract_conformance=5 and security=4 both clear the >=3 thresholds; no test fails. The W6 read-API surface conforms to api.openapi.yaml v1.1.0 (correct EventRow shape, every filter, analytics §7 top-level keys incl. by_model+usage) and degrades to empty-200 with no active run (never 500); per-attempt llm_call emits two correlated rows on a forced failure and one on success; real token usage flows from an openai-style response into adapter.last_usage AND the llm_call payload while Mock stays null; and the trace-field truncation fix truncates/drops cosmetic fields so a verbose model keeps its turn while structural args still fail. W6 gate is GREEN.
